title: EDIT: Previous studies argue that a paradigm shift is needed to expand our understanding of how to address the challenge…

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: EDIT: Previous studies argue that a paradigm shift is needed to expand our understanding of how to address the challenges of strategic alignment in the face of the emerging trend of digitalization in primary industries, including healthcare (Yeow et al., 2018; Kahre et al., 2017; Manfreda & Indihar Štemberger, 2019). Despite the fact that digital technologies in hospitals increase the organizational value and performance, several IT investments fail, due to the lack of strategic alignment(Kitsios et al., 2019). Although alignment has become more relevant with the emergence of new digital technologies in the healthcare industry, previous IS research has not adequately addressed the alignment process in this industry (Yeow et al., 2018).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Several studies have highlighted the need for a paradigm shift in our approach to strategic alignment in response to the growing trend of digitalization in primary industries, such as healthcare (Yeow et al., 2018; Kahre et al., 2017; Manfreda & Indihar Štemberger, 2019). Despite the potential benefits of digital technologies in hospitals, many IT investments fail due to a lack of strategic alignment (Kitsios et al., 2019). However, previous research on strategic alignment in the healthcare industry has been limited (Yeow et al., 2018). Therefore, there is a need for further exploration of the alignment process in the context of digitalization in healthcare.

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: edit: Although alignment has become more relevant with the emergence of new digital technologies in the healthcare industry, previous IS research has not adequately addressed the alignment process in this industry (Yeow et al., 2018).

turns.speaker: assistant
turns.text: Despite the growing importance of alignment in light of the rapid adoption of digital technologies in the healthcare industry, previous IS research has not sufficiently examined the alignment process in this sector (Yeow et al., 2018). Further investigation is needed to better understand the factors and mechanisms that underlie successful alignment between IT investments and organizational strategies in the context of digitalization in healthcare.
